teachingHint: Your child is practising words starting with the consonant cluster /fl/. Two consonant sounds sit next to each other at the start: f then l. Help them blend through: f-l-a-g → flag. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

phonicsWords.teachingTip: f-l-a-g → Blend the opening cluster: fl-a-g → flag

f-l-i-p → Say each sound: /f/-/l/-/i/-/p/. Blend: flip

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! The 'o' says 'u' and the 'e' is silent. We say 'sum'. Learn it by sight.

story: Some flags were flat on the floor. One flag did a flip and landed with a flap!

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! It looks like 'paid' but we say 'sed'. Learn it by sight.

story: A flock of hens flung mud in a flash! One hen said it was time to flog the rug clean.

story: I have some floss to fling at the cat! The cat did flit past the flesh on the dish.
